title: Mirror MCP config union across casing variants instead of merging to one canonical
context: Windows drive letters are case-insensitive, but Claude Code's global config (~/.claude.json) stores projects by raw cwd string with case-sensitive key comparison. A global `claude mcp add` can create two project blocks for the same directory (e.g., `c:/Users/me/repo` vs `C:/Users/me/repo`), and sessions resolving to the non-populated casing see no hunch_* tools. Touches invariant(s): con_8460b6770f (Config/provider writers must merge idempotently into existing user files and refuse to clobber an unparseable file).
decision: When healing a case-split, compute the UNION of MCP servers + enabled/disabled lists across all casing variants, then MIRROR that union back into EVERY casing block. Never delete casings or collapse to a single canonical key.
consequences: Guarantees whichever casing a session resolves to, the MCP config is present — no dead casings
Idempotent: re-running the heal makes no change once merged
Preserves other keys in each project block (merge-only, never clobber)
Backs up ~/.claude.json before any write (timestamped copy)
Only touches MCP-related keys: mcpServers, enabledMcpjsonServers, disabledMcpjsonServers
alternatives_rejected: Collapse to canonical lowercase and delete uppercase casings: sessions resolving to uppercase would get fresh empty blocks re-created by Claude → hunch missing again
Delete both and ask user to re-run `claude mcp add`: destructive, shifts burden to user
Collapse to canonical and rename within the file: same problem — unpredictable which casing wins

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Collapse to canonical lowercase and delete uppercase casings: sessions resolving to uppercase would get fresh empty blocks re-created by Claude → hunch missing again
Delete both and ask user to re-run `claude mcp add`: destructive, shifts burden to user
Collapse to canonical and rename within the file: same problem — unpredictable which casing wins
